front: Novel: the blind monk Jorge of Burgos poisons the pages of Aristotle's lost second book of the Poetics (on comedy) and burns down the labyrinthine library
back: The Name of the Rose (Umberto Eco)

front: Novel: William of Baskerville deduces a missing horse is called Brunellus while investigating murders at a Benedictine abbey in 1327
back: The Name of the Rose (Umberto Eco)

front: Novel: the finis Africae is a secret room in the abbey library; the inquisitor Bernard Gui arrests a peasant girl for witchcraft
back: The Name of the Rose (Umberto Eco)

front: Novel: Venantius's corpse is found in a vat of pig's blood; Adelmo and Berengar are also murdered
back: The Name of the Rose (Umberto Eco)

front: Novel: the cellarer Salvatore repeats "penitenziagite"; William tells Adso the "epoch of penitence is over"
back: The Name of the Rose (Umberto Eco)

front: Novel: three editors -- Belbo, Diotallevi, and Casaubon -- use the computer Abulafia to construct "The Plan," a fictional Knights Templar conspiracy
Novel: based on Colonel Ardenti's manuscript about the Knights Templar; involves telluric currents; divided into ten sections for the ten Sefirot
Novel: Belbo is hanged from the titular scientific instrument in Paris by the Tres Society; the creators come to believe The Plan is true
Novel: a character divides the world into "cretins, fools, morons, and lunatics"
Essay: compares the LBJ Library, waxworks museums, Disneyland, and Superman's Fortress of Solitude as examples of American "faith in fakes"
work: Travels in Hyperreality / How to Travel with a Salmon

front: Essay: the author is charged for minibar alcohol at a hotel after moving the bottles to store a smoked fish he bought in Stockholm
back: "How to Travel with a Salmon" (Umberto Eco)
work: Travels in Hyperreality / How to Travel with a Salmon

front: Essay: lists fourteen features of fascism using Wittgenstein's family resemblance; draws on the author's experience growing up in Mussolini's Italy
Novel: the antisemitic forger Simone Simonini is called "the most hateful man in the world"; Abbe Dalla Piccola alters his diary while he sleeps
Novel: Roberto della Griva is marooned on a ship on the International Date Line; writes love letters to Lady Lilia; seeks the secret of longitude
back: The Island of the Day Before (Umberto Eco)
work: The Island of the Day Before

front: Novel: an Italian peasant adopted by Frederick Barbarossa recounts his fifteen-year search for Prester John to Niketas Choniates after the sack of Constantinople
Novel: the amnesiac Yambo discovers Shakespeare's First Folio among his grandfather's books, reliving lost memories of his childhood
back: The Mysterious Flame of Queen Loana (Umberto Eco)

front: Novel: the paranoiac Braggadocio believes Mussolini survived WWII; hack journalists have an affair; this author's final novel
Author: contrasted classical art with the aleatoric music of Stockhausen and Boulez in The Open Work, extolling "openness" as the central goal of art
Author: argued that texts produce "model readers" in The Role of the Reader
Author: outlined nine possible plot moves in James Bond novels in "Narrative Structures in Fleming"
Author: cited The Count of Monte Cristo as a literary predecessor of the "intertextual archetypes" that made Casablanca a cult film
Author: proclaimed "the list is the origin of culture" in the illustrated essay The Infinity of Lists
